items.prompt: constructs or completes bounded geometric arguments using givens, definitions, prior results, and valid reasons
items.expected_evidence: complete or critique short geometric arguments with coherent claim-evidence flow
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner produces a structurally coherent bounded proof or proof completion with valid supporting reasons.

items.prompt: explains why a bounded geometric argument is valid, including how givens, definitions, and theorem use connect to the conclusion
items.expected_evidence: supply valid reasons for bounded geometric statements and theorem uses
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains proof as a chain of justified claims rather than a template to copy.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to visual_assertion_substitution.
items.expected_evidence: critiques incomplete or invalid arguments and repairs bounded proof gaps
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where a visual claim replaced a justified step or where a reason does not support the statement.

items.prompt: builds or critiques a bounded geometric argument in a less-familiar diagram or mixed algebra-geometry setting
items.expected_evidence: connect algebraic manipulation and geometric properties inside a single bounded proof
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent justification across geometry and algebra in a varied proof situation.
